Off Calendar Jun-24-2024 Continued To Jul-01-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-09-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

This matter was put on for hearing with no appearance required for the purpose of admitting the will. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed. The will dated June 14, 2018 is admitted to probate. Dru Parker is appointed as the Administrator of the estate with full authority. Bond is fixed at $2,627,000.00. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for November 18, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status on administration is filed two weeks prior to the hearing date, the status hearing will go off calendar. Shirley Yawitz is appointed Probate Referee.

Judge: Ross Moody: Clerk: A. Datt: Deputy: Napata: Not reported.

Diann Montemayor?S Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement Re: Will Contest
Off Calendar Aug-22-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Timothy Chambers appeared by zoom for Diann Montemayor (petitioner/objector).
Jeff Hall appeared by zoom for Scott Fisher and Debrah Denoble (objectors).

The court noted that no one is here to object to the settlement petition. Discussion was held regarding the terms of the settlement agreement. Mr. Hall stated that he prefers to have an order indicating that the settlement agreement is approved and then have his client's petition go off calendar, and that he is happy to file a dismissal within 14-days of the order being signed which is called for in the settlement agreement. The Petition for Approval of Settlement Agreement Re: Will Contest is under submission with the intent to grant. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary (Mrs. Montemayor's petition) is continued to September 18, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of an amended petition. The Petition for Letters of Administration (Mrs. Denoble's petition) is also continued to September 18, 2023. The amended petition is to be given a hearing date of September 18, 2023.
Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jan-18-2023 Continued To May-31-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-18-2023 Continued To May-31-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom: Timothy Chambers, Esq. for DiAnn Montemayor. Michelle Nguyen, Esq. for Deborah DeNoble and Scott Fisher.

Mr. Chambers stated that they have completed quite a bit of discovery including depositions, that the parties agreed to mediation, and that they are requesting a continuance to allow time to complete the mediation. Ms. Nguyen concurred with Mr. Chambers comments and requested a continuance date four to six months out.

At the request of the parties, the Petition for Probate of Will and Letters Testamentary and the Petition for Letters of Administration are continued to May 31, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Status on mediation is set for May 31, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. Counsel are to provide an update for the court. A joint statement is to be filed by May 24, 2023.

Ms. Nguyen stated that an order extending Dru Parker's status as special administrator was submitted to the Court. The Court confirmed receipt of the order and stated that it would attempt to have the order signed by the following day.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: H. Singh. Matter not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-28-2022 Continued To Jan-18-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-28-2022 Continued To Jan-18-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Timothy Chambers, via Zoom, for DiAnn Montemayor, Petitioner; Michelle Nguyen, via Zoom, for Deborah DeNoble and Scott Fisher, Objectors.

The court queries counsel whether they have met and conferred regarding a roadmap for resolution. Mr. Chambers reports of preliminary written discovery to which he has received responses to and that there has been some meet and confer but doesn't believe the matter is in position to discuss mediation. Ms. Nguyen agrees with Mr. Chambers' report and advises of pending subpoenas. The court acknowledges the position of this matter and that it will need to be continued. Counsel is advised to contact the court any time prior to the next hearing in this matter if they wish to be referred to the court's pro bono mediation program. Ms. Nguyen advises that Letters of Special Administration expire in December. Counsel is instructed to deliver new Letters to the court with an expiration date to match the date of the next calling of this matter.

The matters are continued to Jan-18-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

***After Hearing (Sep-29-2022): Mr. Chambers instructed by the court to deliver a proposed order RE Special Administration to the court along with the Letters of Special Administration as mentioned above.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-03-2022 Continued To Sep-28-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Timothy Chambers, via Zoom, for Diann Montemayor, Petitioner; Michelle Nguyen, via Zoom, for Scott Fisher and Deborah DeNoble, Objectors.

The court points to the apparent issue of improper service on heir Jeffery Mulhall. Ms. Nguyen reports of attempts to locate Mr. Mulhall and believes that he may be deceased. Mr. Chambers reports that he does not know whether Mr. Mulhall is alive and that he spoke with an attorney familiar with Mr. Mulhall who advised him that she had hired an heir-search company who advises that Mr. Mulhall is alive. The court advises that it is not inclined to proceed without confirmation of service on Mr. Mulhall. The court queries counsel regarding whether they anticipate contesting Laverne's will. Ms. Nguyen advises that she does not intend to contest Laverne's will.

After additional discussion, the petitions set for hearing are continued to Sep-28-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.
